If 50% reach the store but only 5% view an offer, the store layout needs improvement. If 8% initiate purchase but only 3% complete, the payment flow has friction.

rubric.key: Correctly defines funnel purpose as identifying drop-off points
Five stages are reasonable and ordered correctly
Provides example of actionable insight from funnel data

questions.question: Describe the service locator pattern as applied to cross-platform mobile game development, and explain how it solves the problem of platform-specific IAP implementations on iOS and Android.
questions.answer: The service locator pattern uses a central registry that maps interface types to concrete implementations. For cross-platform IAP: define an IIAPService interface with methods like PurchaseProduct(string productId) and RestorePurchases(). Create AppleIAPService (wrapping StoreKit 2) and GoogleIAPService (wrapping Play Billing Library 6.0). At app startup, register the appropriate implementation: on iOS, ServiceLocator.Register<IIAPService>(new AppleIAPService()); on Android, ServiceLocator.Register<IIAPService>(new GoogleIAPService()). All game code calls ServiceLocator.Get<IIAPService>().PurchaseProduct(id) without knowing which platform it is running on. Platform-specific code is confined to two places: the concrete service implementations and the startup registration. This prevents conditional compilation (#if UNITY_IOS) from being scattered throughout the codebase.

rubric.key: Correctly describes the service locator registry concept
Shows interface abstraction with concrete platform implementations
Identifies benefit of isolating platform-specific code

questions.question: Explain the difference between a remote config change and an addressable content update for a live mobile game, and describe when you would use each.
questions.answer: Remote config changes modify key-value parameters (numbers, strings, booleans) that the game reads at runtime. Changes take effect within minutes when the game fetches the latest config. Use for: tuning difficulty multipliers, enabling/disabling features with flags, changing event schedules, adjusting IAP prices, A/B testing parameter values. No new content is downloaded -- only small JSON values change. Addressable content updates deliver new binary assets (textures, models, audio, levels) by uploading updated asset bundles to a CDN and updating the content catalog. The game downloads new bundles as needed. Use for: adding new characters, levels, seasonal cosmetics, or fixing art bugs. Requires the player to download data (possibly on WiFi). Use remote config for tuning existing behavior and addressable updates for delivering new content.

rubric.key: Correctly distinguishes parameter tuning from content delivery
Appropriate use cases for remote config
Appropriate use cases for addressable content with download consideration

questions.question: What is a soft-launch, and describe the three KPIs you would evaluate before deciding to proceed to a worldwide launch.
questions.answer: A soft-launch is releasing the game in a limited market (typically 1-3 countries like Canada, Australia, or Philippines) to gather real player data before committing to a global launch with marketing spend. The three KPIs: (1) D1 retention: must exceed 40% for casual, 30% for mid-core. Below these thresholds, the first-time user experience needs work before spending on acquisition. (2) D7 retention: must exceed 15-20%. Indicates the core loop sustains engagement beyond novelty. (3) LTV vs projected CPI: the estimated LTV from soft-launch monetization data must exceed the projected CPI for the target global markets by a factor of at least 1.5-2x to account for overhead. If any of these are below threshold, iterate on the game and re-evaluate before launching globally.

rubric.key: Correctly defines soft-launch with market selection
Three KPIs are appropriate with reasonable threshold values
Explains the iterate-before-launching decision framework

questions.question: A player is in the middle of a boss fight when they receive a phone call. Describe the sequence of app lifecycle events on iOS, what the game must do at each step, and what should happen when the player returns to the game.
questions.answer: Lifecycle sequence on iOS: (1) applicationWillResignActive: the app is about to lose focus. The game should immediately pause gameplay, save the current state to persistent storage (boss health, player position, active effects), and mute audio. (2) applicationDidEnterBackground: the app is now in the background. Reduce memory footprint if possible. The OS may terminate the app at any time from this point without further notice. (3) When the player finishes the call, applicationWillEnterForeground is called, followed by applicationDidBecomeActive. The game should restore from the saved state, resume audio, and display the pause menu (do not auto-resume gameplay). The player should see the exact game state from when the call arrived and choose when to unpause. If the OS terminated the app during the call, the next launch should detect the saved mid-gameplay state and offer to resume from the save point.

rubric.key: Correct lifecycle event sequence on iOS
Appropriate actions at each lifecycle stage (save, pause, mute)
Handles both return-from-background and terminated-by-OS cases

questions.question: List three common reasons for Apple App Store review rejection that are specific to games, and describe how to avoid each one.
questions.answer: (1) Crashes during review: the reviewer tests on devices the developer may not have tested on, or the game crashes on first launch due to server connectivity issues during review. Avoid: test on multiple device models including the oldest supported, ensure the game handles server unavailability gracefully (offline mode or clear error message), and verify the demo account credentials work. (2) Undisclosed loot box/gacha odds: Apple requires apps with randomized virtual item purchases to disclose the odds of receiving each item before purchase. Avoid: display drop rates for all randomized reward systems before the player can spend money, and include this in the review notes. (3) Misleading screenshots: store screenshots that show gameplay or graphics not representative of the actual game. Avoid: capture screenshots from the actual game build being submitted, label any aspirational or cinematic content clearly, and ensure screenshots show real gameplay.

rubric.key: Three distinct game-specific rejection reasons
Prevention strategies are specific and actionable
Reasons are accurately attributed to Apple's guidelines

questions.question: After launching a mobile game, your D7 retention drops from 18% to 12% following a content update. Describe how you would use analytics to diagnose the cause and what immediate actions you would take.
questions.answer: Diagnosis with analytics: (1) Compare cohort behavior: separate players who updated from those on the old version. If old-version players maintain 18% D7, the update caused the drop. (2) Examine session data: check if session length or sessions-per-day decreased after the update, indicating less engaging gameplay. (3) Check level/feature funnels: identify if the new content introduced a progression wall or frustration point where players drop off. (4) Review crash data: check if the update introduced new crashes that are terminating sessions. (5) Check the last event before churn for the new cohort. Immediate actions: (1) If a crash is detected, prepare an emergency hotfix and request expedited review. (2) If a difficulty spike is found, use remote config to reduce difficulty of the problematic content without an app update. (3) If the cause is unclear, halt any active user acquisition campaigns to stop paying to acquire users who will churn. (4) Communicate with the community about known issues.

rubric.key: Systematic analytics diagnosis approach with cohort comparison
Identifies multiple possible causes and how to distinguish them
Immediate actions are appropriate and prioritized

major_assignments.title: Mobile-Optimized Game Prototype
major_assignments.description: Design and build a fully playable mobile game prototype that demonstrates mastery of touch input design, mobile UI patterns, and performance optimization. The game must run at a stable 60fps on a mid-range mobile device, implement at least two distinct touch interaction mechanics (e.g., virtual joystick + tap-to-shoot, swipe + pinch), feature a responsive mobile UI that adapts to different screen sizes and safe areas, and include basic Firebase Analytics integration tracking session and level events.
objectives: Implement intuitive touch-based controls with proper dead zones and haptic feedback
Design a mobile-native UI that adapts to different screen sizes, aspect ratios, and safe areas
Optimize rendering and memory usage to sustain 60fps on mid-range target hardware
Integrate Firebase Analytics to track session starts, level completions, and custom engagement events
deliverables: Unity project with iOS and Android build targets configured
APK or IPA build tested on a physical mobile device
Performance profiling report with frame time, memory, and draw call analysis (2-3 pages)
Technical document describing input system architecture and UI responsive design (3-4 pages)

criteria: Touch controls feel responsive with proper dead zones and gesture disambiguation
Multi-touch interactions work without conflicts or dropped inputs
Analytics events are logged correctly and visible in Firebase dashboard
Code is well-structured with clear input handling separation
UI respects safe areas on notched and punch-hole devices
Touch targets meet minimum size guidelines (44pt iOS / 48dp Android)
UI scales correctly across phone and tablet aspect ratios
Visual feedback for every interactive element (press states, animations)
Sustains 60fps on target mid-range device (profiling evidence required)
Memory usage stays under 400MB on device
No GC allocation spikes above 1KB per frame during gameplay
Textures use ASTC compression, draw calls are batched appropriately
Input design rationale explains each control scheme decision
Profiling data presented with analysis and optimization narrative
UI responsive design approach documented with multi-device screenshots
Professional formatting and writing
Polished touch controls that feel native to mobile
Rock-solid 60fps performance on mid-range hardware with profiling evidence
Thoughtful mobile UI that adapts to device variations
Analytics integration proving player behavior tracking works

major_assignments.title: Monetized Mobile Game with Analytics
major_assignments.description: Extend the prototype from Assignment 1 with a complete monetization layer, analytics-driven insights, and engagement systems. Implement at least one IAP flow with server-side receipt validation, a rewarded ad placement, daily reward system, and push notification for at least one game event. The app store listing (on a test track) must be prepared with optimized metadata.
objectives: Implement a complete IAP flow with server-side receipt validation
Integrate rewarded video ads with player-friendly placement
Build a daily reward system with streak tracking and notification
Prepare an optimized app store listing on a test track
deliverables: Updated Unity project with IAP, ads, engagement systems, and notifications
Server-side receipt validation endpoint (Firebase Cloud Function or equivalent)
App store listing on TestFlight or Google Play Internal Testing track
Monetization strategy document with analytics plan, KPI targets, and ASO analysis (3-4 pages)

criteria: IAP flow completes successfully in sandbox with server-side validation
Rewarded ad integration works with proper loading, showing, and reward delivery
Daily reward system tracks streaks and persists across sessions
Push notifications fire for at least one game event (timer completion, daily reward)
Store/shop UI clearly distinguishes free and premium content
Purchase confirmation flow prevents accidental purchases
Daily reward calendar is visually clear and motivating
App store listing screenshots show real gameplay with benefit captions
Ad SDK integration does not cause frame rate drops during gameplay
IAP flow handles errors gracefully (network loss, validation failure)
Analytics event volume does not impact game performance
Notification permission is requested at an appropriate moment
Monetization strategy explains model choice with expected KPI targets
ASO analysis includes keyword research and competitive comparison
Analytics event taxonomy is documented with rationale for each event
Ethical considerations are addressed (no predatory mechanics, disclosed odds)
Complete monetization pipeline from IAP to validation to delivery
Analytics integration that provides actionable business insights
Engagement systems that create genuine reasons to return
Professional-quality app store listing ready for public launch

major_assignments.title: Live Operations and Cross-Platform Deployment
major_assignments.description: Add live operations infrastructure to the game: remote configuration for live tuning, addressable content for a downloadable content pack, a time-limited event system driven by server timestamps, and cross-platform testing across at least two devices. Demonstrate a live parameter change affecting the running game in real time.
objectives: Implement Firebase Remote Config for live game parameter tuning
Build an addressable content pack that downloads new content without an app update
Create a time-limited event system driven by server-controlled timestamps
Verify cross-platform functionality on at least two different devices or platforms
deliverables: Unity project with Remote Config, Addressables, and event system integrated
Remote addressable content hosted on Firebase Hosting or equivalent CDN
Video demonstrating a live Remote Config change affecting the running game
Cross-platform test report documenting behavior on two devices (2 pages)

criteria: Remote Config fetches, activates, and applies values correctly
Addressable content downloads and loads without errors
Event system activates and deactivates based on server timestamps
Graceful fallback when remote services are unreachable
Event UI clearly communicates time remaining and rewards
Downloaded content integrates seamlessly with existing art style
Loading states for content downloads are clear and non-blocking
UI handles the event appearing and disappearing without layout breaks
Content download does not block gameplay
Remote Config fetch does not add visible startup delay
Addressable content cache is managed properly (not re-downloading unchanged content)
Cross-platform performance is acceptable on both test devices
Live ops architecture is documented with sequence diagrams
Remote Config parameter list documented with default values and ranges
Cross-platform test report documents device-specific behaviors and differences
Content update workflow is documented for team handoff
Functional live ops that demonstrates real-time parameter tuning
Working content delivery pipeline without app store update
Event system that creates urgency and engagement
Cross-platform deployment verified on real devices

weekly_quizzes.description: Five quizzes covering mobile platforms, performance, monetization, analytics, live ops, and post-launch management

lab_assignments.description: Weekly hands-on exercises in touch input, profiling, IAP integration, analytics setup, and store listing preparation

major_assignments.description: Three major projects: mobile prototype, monetized game with analytics, and live operations deployment

midterm_examination.description: Comprehensive assessment of mobile platforms, touch input, performance optimization, and monetization concepts

final_project.description: Complete mobile game published to a test track with analytics, monetization, live operations, and performance optimization

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all lab sessions and device testing workshops. More than 2 unexcused absences may result in a reduced final grade. Device testing sessions require advance scheduling.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments are penalized 10% per day. No submissions accepted more than 5 days late without prior arrangement. All build submissions must compile, install, and run on a physical mobile device.
course_policies.collaboration: Individual assignments must be completed independently. Students may share device testing resources but all code, design, and documentation must be original or properly attributed.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All code must be original or properly attributed. Use of AI code generation tools must be disclosed. Submitting purchased or copied Asset Store templates as original work will result in course failure.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students must have access to a physical iOS or Android device for testing. Lab computers have Xcode and Android Studio available. A personal Apple Developer account ($99/year) is recommended for TestFlight distribution but not required.
